commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add delete button for contact messages in admin panels

Both /admin/mensajes and /admin/contacto now have a red "Eliminar"
button with JS confirmation. FK CASCADE handles related records.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Optimize agent token footprint — extract templates to references, add structural hooks

Two-build optimization that reduces agent file sizes by ~33% (4800→3127 lines)
and moves mechanical enforcement from instructions to hooks.

Build 1 — Executor optimization (1485→879 lines, 41% reduction):
- Extract build plan, experience report, CLAUDE.md templates → references/executor-templates.md
- Extract build trace template → references/build-trace-template.md
- Extract context fidelity modes, budget gate → references/context-management.md
- Create hooks/auto-version.js: PostToolUse hook auto-increments patch version
  after chunk commits during active builds (was instruction-based, often skipped)
- Create hooks/emit-event.sh: shared event emission utility replacing ~70 lines
  of inline boilerplate per agent (dual-path: viewer API + state.json fallback)

Build 2 — State Owner, Interviewer, Observer, Spec Writer optimization:
- State Owner (944→479, 49%): classification table, briefing format, readiness
  values, drift/untracked formats → references/state-owner-templates.md
- Interviewer (576→342, 41%): 8 phase question lists, state template, experience
  language guide, digest format → references/interview-templates.md
- Observer (505→334, 34%): verdict/report formats, viewer discovery, event schema,
  audit trail → references/observer-templates.md
- Spec Writer (472→400, 15%): interchange schemas consolidated
- Global memory management (~200 lines) → references/memory-protocol.md
- All agent interchange schemas consolidated → references/interchange-schema.md
- Workflow prerequisite blocks across 6 agents deduplicated to 3-line pointers
  to canonical table in references/state-protocol.md

Progressive disclosure: agent files carry only core judgment logic; templates
and formats load on demand from reference files. Hooks provide structural
enforcement that can't be skipped under context pressure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

filename normalizer script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Detect zombie notary processes and add TCP liveness probe to watchdog

is_running() now checks /proc/{pid}/status for zombie state (Z), not
just returncode. The watchdog also probes the notary port via TCP every
30 seconds. A process that exists but isn't accepting connections gets
killed and restarted.

Implement complete image upload flow with drag-drop and paste support. Images are committed to the repo and inserted with blob URLs for instant preview while maintaining relative markdown paths. Add repo-specific settings page with gear icon accessible from sidebar, replace require-PR checkbox with toggle, hide organize-by-folder setting behind Advanced collapsible. - Add imageStorageFolder, imageUrlPrefix, organizeByFolder to RepoSettings schema
- Implement POST /api/github/[owner]/[repo]/upload-image route with multipart form handling
- Create EditorImage extension with data-markdown-src attribute for markdown paths
- Add ImageUploadPlaceholder node for in-flight uploads with spinner

- Use blob URLs for preview + custom Turndown rule for markdown output
- Add settingsOpen state guard to prevent saves during uploads
- Show first-upload info hint (localStorage-dismissed per repo)

who was clearly visible in the image.

The Sync Now button silently swallowed errors and gave no feedback on
success. Users had no way to know whether the sync worked, failed, or
simply found nothing new. Now shows toast notifications for all three
outcomes with the actual error message from the backend.

Also quotes the sheet name in the Google Sheets API URL (A1 notation
requires single quotes for names with spaces or special characters).

repository.description: Fair Lawn, NJ local service apps — community center, library, and pool

commit.message:
feat: 5th optional for 7th/6th chords, sandwichedOpen penalty, mMaj7 parse

- Fifth is now optional when chord contains 7th or 6th (R-3-7 shell is
  standard guitar voicing, not just with tensions/9th+)
- Add sandwichedOpen penalty (-25): open string between fretted strings
  causes vibration clash — uncommon technique on guitar
- Fix FmMaj7/CmM7 parsing: add mMaj7/mM7 to PAD_QUALITY_INTERVALS and

commit.message:
feat(alert): add drift alert debounce to suppress transient flaps (#94)

* feat(alert): add drift alert debounce to suppress transient flaps

Add a configurable debounce layer to the drift alert pipeline that
suppresses alerts until drift persists beyond a configurable window.
This eliminates noise from self-healing transients like Unraid mover
I/O pressure, Watchtower image updates, and Docker daemon restarts.

Pipeline flow: detect -> debounce filter -> dedup (per-item cooldown) -> send

- Add DriftDebounceItems map to DeployState for persistence
- Add FilterDebounced() pure function in reconcile/state.go
- Add BOSUN_DRIFT_ALERT_DEBOUNCE env var (duration-or-bare-seconds)
- Add drift_alert_debounce config key in bosun.yaml
- Integrate debounce into daemon drift check loop
- Resolution alerts bypass debounce (fire immediately for alerted items,
  skip items still in debounce window)
- Default 0 (disabled) for backwards compatibility

Closes #62

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(daemon): track explicit env var for drift debounce precedence

When BOSUN_DRIFT_ALERT_DEBOUNCE is set to "0" explicitly, the previous
logic used cfg.DriftAlertDebounce == 0 to decide whether to apply the
project config value. Since 0 is both a valid "disabled" setting and the
Go zero value for unset, this caused the config file value to override
the explicit env var.

Track whether the env var was explicitly set via a boolean flag, matching
the existing PostSyncHooksFromEnv pattern. Add a test that verifies env
var "0" prevents the config file value from taking effect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(daemon): run drift cleanup unconditionally and reject negative debounce

- Remove HasCriticalDrift() gate on drift cleanup path so resolved
  items are cleared even when only non-critical drift remains
- Reject negative BOSUN_DRIFT_ALERT_DEBOUNCE values with a warning

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(daemon): allow zero debounce duration (means disabled)

Change guard from d <= 0 to d < 0 so explicit zero is accepted
as "debounce disabled" per the spec.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(daemon): skip debounce for already-alerted drift items

Items already in DriftAlertedItems should flow directly to the
dedup/cooldown layer via ShouldAlertDrift, not re-enter debounce.
Prevents graduated items from being withheld on subsequent checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(daemon): log graduation only for debounce-pending items

Iterate pendingItems instead of criticalItems so already-alerted
items don't get logged as "graduated from debounce".

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Cameron Sjo <cameronsjo@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
EMI-142: Retry merge failures instead of permanently failing tasks (#129)

When mergePr() fails, keep the task in awaiting_ci rather than
immediately setting it to failed and writing Cancelled to Linear.
This prevents the cascade that destroys completed, approved PRs.

Changes:
- Add merge_attempt_count column to tasks (DB migration 9)
- In mergeAndFinalize(), on merge failure: increment attempt count
  and return early (keeping awaiting_ci state). The CI poll loop
  will call mergeAndFinalize again on the next scheduler tick.
- After 3 consecutive merge failures, escalate: set failed internally
  but write In Review (not Cancelled) to Linear so the PR stays open.
  Post a comment explaining the blocker and asking for human intervention.
- Reset mergeAttemptCount to 0 on user-initiated Backlog/Todo resets
  so a fresh start gets the full retry budget.

This fixes the incident where EMI-84, EMI-115, and EMI-129 all had
approved PRs destroyed by the failed -> Cancelled -> closePRs cascade
triggered by transient BEHIND/merge-blocked failures.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
InstagramリンクURLを修正（hoyakenittekouwai）

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(reconcile): classify compose failures to tolerate unhealthy containers (#92)

* fix(reconcile): classify compose failures to tolerate unhealthy containers

When `docker compose up` exits non-zero, inspect container state via
`docker compose ps --format json` to distinguish unhealthy containers
from genuine start failures. Unhealthy containers produce warnings
instead of triggering rollback, preventing unnecessary deployment
failures caused by pre-existing unhealthy containers (e.g., obsidian
with 21,553 consecutive healthcheck failures blocking ALL deployments).

Changes:
- Add ErrComposeUnhealthy sentinel error for recoverable failures
- Add classifyComposePS pure function to categorize container states
- Add classifyComposeFailure/classifyComposeFailureRemote for local/SSH
- ComposeUpMultiple now classifies failures before returning
- ComposeUpMultipleWithRollback skips rollback on ErrComposeUnhealthy
- ComposeUpRemote applies equivalent classification for SSH deployments
- deployLocal treats ErrComposeUnhealthy as warning, not pipeline failure

Closes #37

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(reconcile): address CodeRabbit findings on compose failure classification

- Add injectable composeUpFn seam for testing ErrComposeUnhealthy path
- Add --all flag to docker compose ps for stopped container visibility
- Support both JSON array and NDJSON formats in parseComposePSOutput
- Return failureStartFailure when no containers are unhealthy or failed

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(reconcile): wire VerifyContainerHealth with compose PS parser

Use parseComposePSOutput and classifyComposePS to actually inspect
container state instead of returning nil on success.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(reconcile): make failureStartFailure the iota zero value

Ensures uninitialized composeFailureResult defaults to the
conservative rollback path (fail-safe) rather than skip-rollback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(reconcile): treat "starting" health status as unhealthy

Containers still in health check startup should not be classified as
healthy. Adds test case for the "starting" health state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Cameron Sjo <cameronsjo@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement v2.5 goals (SIEM stub, member alerting, shared ACE helper)

Shared ACE-matching helper (Find-GRAce.ps1):
- Extracts the duplicated ACE-matching loop from 4 modules into a single
  helper that accepts a pre-resolved SID string and ACL object
- No AD calls in the helper -- makes ACE-matching logic independently
  testable without a live domain connection
- All 4 modules self-source the helper via $PSScriptRoot at load time
- Get-GRReport.ps1 dot-sources the helper and removes its local copy
- Tests/Find-GRAce.Tests.ps1: 12 new unit tests (34 total, 0 AD required)

Get-GRReport.ps1 additions:
- Membership change alerting: auto-baseline CSV in Logs/; compares on every
  run; writes to Windows Application event log (EventId 8650, source
  'AD-Global-Reader') and console on delta; -RefreshBaseline to acknowledge
- Logon activity section: queries PDC Security event log for 4624 events
  matching group member accounts (last 7 days, up to 200 events);
  -SiemEndpoint displays sample SPL/KQL/EQL queries instead; access-denied
  and WinRM failures surface a clear 'not configured' notice

Bug fixes:
- Set-GR-AdminSDHolder.ps1: replace $domain.PDCEmulator in TriggerSDProp
  block with (Get-ADDomain).PDCEmulator to eliminate stale outer-scope risk
  (Remove-GR-AdminSDHolder already used this pattern)

Verify-Deployment.ps1 updated for v2:
- Uses shared Find-GRAce helper for all ACE lookups
- Adds -CheckAdminSDHolder parameter (informational; absence not a failure)
- Adds proper synopsis/description/example help block
- Exits with code 1 on failure

Other:
- Sign-GRScripts.ps1: adds Find-GRAce.ps1 to the signing manifest
- V2-Decisions.md: v2.5 section documenting all design decisions above
- README.md: updated Get-GRReport parameters table and Verify-Deployment
  section; adds Find-GRAce.ps1 to file structure

Test results: 34/34 unit tests passing, 29/29 integration tests passing
